body: The average of many independent random variables converges to a normal distribution — regardless of the original distribution. It's why the bell curve appears everywhere, from test scores to measurement errors.
Start with a prior belief, observe evidence, update to a posterior. Bayes' theorem formalizes how rational agents should revise beliefs — prior × likelihood, then normalize.
In a room of just 23 people, there's a ~50% chance two share a birthday. By 70 people it's 99.9%. Our intuition drastically underestimates how quickly collision probabilities grow.
In leader-follower replication, followers may lag behind the leader. Reads from a stale follower can return outdated data — a key trade-off when scaling reads horizontally.
The probability of observing data at least as extreme as yours, assuming the null hypothesis is true. It does not tell you the probability the null is true — a very common misreading.
Switching doors after the host reveals a goat wins 2/3 of the time. Sticking wins only 1/3. Your initial pick was made when all three doors were uncertain; new information changes the odds.
Before any change is written to the data file, it's appended to a log. On crash recovery, the log is replayed. WAL is the foundation of durability in most relational databases.
A trend that appears in several groups of data can reverse or disappear when the groups are combined. Always ask which confounding variable might be lurking behind an aggregate statistic.
A distributed system can guarantee at most two of: Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance. Since network partitions are unavoidable, real systems trade off between C and A.
As sample size grows, the sample mean converges to the true expected value. Gambling houses rely on this: over enough rounds, house edge becomes near-certain profit.
We study the survivors because the failures have vanished from the data. WWII planes were reinforced where returning aircraft showed damage — the missing planes took hits elsewhere.
A technique for distributing keys across nodes so that when a node is added or removed, only a fraction of keys need to move. Essential for elastic scaling in distributed caches and databases.
After an extreme measurement, the next tends to be closer to average — not because of any intervention, but pure statistics. It's why 'slumps' often self-correct and why we over-credit coaches.
The probability-weighted average of all possible outcomes. A lottery ticket with a $1 expected value for a $2 price is a bad bet every time — even if one ticket once wins millions.
A protocol for atomic distributed transactions: coordinator asks all nodes to prepare (phase 1), then commits only if all agree (phase 2). Consistent but blocks if the coordinator crashes mid-way.
